items.summary: The micro-plan study/plan review pages and group page set create_solicitation_url = /solicitations/create/?source_program_id=<pid>&source_plan_id=<plan>|source_group_id=<grp>, surfaced as a 'Create solicitation' button.
items.claim_hint: A program owner can launch a solicitation directly from an approved micro-plan, carrying the plan as the source.

items.summary: The 'Create solicitation' button renders on the study group + plan review pages (verified live on labs prod: plan 4494 review page shows the button with href source_plan_id=4494).
items.claim_hint: The recruit-a-firm action is reachable from the plan the owner just designed — no separate tool, same workspace.

items.summary: The create view snapshots the source plan via ProgramPlanDataAccess into plans_json/source_plan_ids_json; verified live the form pre-seeds title, scope_of_work, and plans_json={plan_id:4494, name:'R6 — Attakar × Gura'}.
items.claim_hint: The R6 plan rides into the solicitation as a snapshotted coverage area at creation time (later plan edits don't change this solicitation).

items.summary: The create form shows a 'Coverage areas (from micro-plan)' panel listing the snapshotted plans, with helper text that respondents select the ones they can cover (verified live: coverage_panel=true, plan 'R6 — Attakar' listed).
items.claim_hint: The owner sees and confirms the coverage area the firm will bid on, before publishing.

items.summary: When a solicitation carries plans, the public response form adds a 'Which areas can you cover?' CheckboxSelectMultiple bound to the snapshot plans.
items.claim_hint: An independent survey firm reads the public solicitation and selects the specific coverage area(s) it can staff.

items.summary: On submit, the response stores selected_plan_ids/selected_plan_names resolved against the solicitation's authoritative snapshot; submission is blocked unless at least one coverage area is selected.
items.claim_hint: The firm's application is bound to a concrete coverage area, captured for the reviewer — not a free-text claim.

items.summary: The R6 'Attakar × Gura' two-arm study is a single plan (id 4494) whose input_areas carry both wards arm-tagged (Attakar=intervention 403 WA, Gura=comparison 437 WA), sampled with the shared size-balanced config (840 work areas, 596 primary/244 alternate).
items.claim_hint: The coverage area offered to the firm is the exact two-arm sample the household survey must run — the same plan verified-monitoring R6 consumes.

items.ref: .canopy/ddd/context.md (## This run + Planning vs execution boundary) + scripts/walkthroughs/verified-monitoring/demo_config.json
items.summary: The R6 plan grounds the verified-monitoring Round 6 household-survey records (68.1% verified vs 8.9% self-report hero round, ~300 households/round). The survey firm is the recruitment step that was previously only narrative framing.
items.claim_hint: Recruiting the firm is the missing middle that connects the design demo (study-design) to the monitoring demo (verified-monitoring).

items.ref: grep: no provision/create_opportunity/WorkArea-push in commcare_connect/solicitations/ (only award_response in data_access.py)
items.summary: v1 captures the response for review; there is NO code that, on award, provisions a Connect opportunity or pushes the plan's work areas into a live opp's WorkArea set. The award flow is untouched by PR #616.
items.claim_hint: Award -> provision a Connect opportunity -> enumerators actually run R6 is a DEFERRED capability gap (aspirational end-state), not a shipped mechanism.

items.summary: Before PR #618, SolicitationsDataAccess only passed program_id (not opportunity_id) to LabsRecordAPIClient, so labs-only programs (10008) hit the prod API and 404'd; create-from-plan failed on the demo program. PR #618 routes labs-only programs to the local-records backend.
items.claim_hint: The shipped create-from-plan flow now actually persists on the synthetic demo program it is demonstrated on.

items.summary: Tests assert the review and group contexts emit create_solicitation_url with the source_program_id + source_plan_id/source_group_id contract.
items.claim_hint: The create-from-plan entry point is contract-tested, not incidental.
